title.en: Ensure noexec option set on /var/log/audit partition

prose.en: Ensure noexec option set on /var/log/audit partition

description.en: The `noexec` mount option specifies that the filesystem cannot contain executable binaries.

rationale.en: Since the `/var/log/audit` filesystem is only intended for audit logs, set this option to ensure that users cannot run executable binaries from `/var/log/audit`.

audit.en:
**\- IF \-** a separate partition exists for `/var/log/audit`.  

Edit the `/etc/fstab` file and add `noexec` to the fourth field (mounting options) for the `/var/log/audit` partition.  

*Example:*  

Run the following command to remount `/var/log/audit` with the configured options:

```
# mount -o remount /var/log/audit
```

```
 /var/log/audit         defaults,rw,nosuid,nodev,noexec,relatime  0 0
```

remediation.en:
**\- IF \-** a separate partition exists for `/var/log/audit`.  

Edit the `/etc/fstab` file and add `noexec` to the fourth field (mounting options) for the `/var/log/audit` partition.  

*Example:*  

Run the following command to remount `/var/log/audit` with the configured options:

```
# mount -o remount /var/log/audit
```

```
 /var/log/audit         defaults,rw,nosuid,nodev,noexec,relatime  0 0
```

references: See the fstab(5) manual page for more information.
